claim: Deep agents are characterized by having a planning tool, ability to delegate to subagents, file system for storing information, and long detailed system prompts
actionable: When designing deep agents, ensure they have explicit planning capabilities, subagent spawning, persistent file storage, and comprehensive system context

claim: Deep agents file systems allow agents to write output for later reference, enable targeted edits on specific lines, and allow critique agents to flag necessary changes
actionable: Implement persistent file storage in agents to enable intermediate review, editing, and critique workflows

claim: Deep agents maintain todo lists that track task status and can be visually represented in a dedicated UI workspace pane for transparency
actionable: Build todo-list interfaces for deep agents to provide visibility into planned work, current status, and completed tasks

claim: Subagents in deep agent systems receive exact input parameters and eventually return output that can be inspected by clicking into subtask entries
actionable: Design deep agent UIs to provide inspection capabilities for subagent inputs and outputs to enable debugging and verification
